title: Unable to run Podman on gitpod
body:
Have been looking into getting Podman to run on gitpod, but for some reason this fails. I use the following repo to get this started: https://github.com/gbraad/gitpod-template-podman/. You can easily open and verify this with https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/gbraad/gitpod-template-podman

The error I get is that both Rootless and rooted does not work. Below are the errors:
```
gitpod /workspace/gitpod-template-podman (master) $ podman run alpine
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers 
Error: cannot setup namespace using newuidmap: exit status 1
gitpod /workspace/gitpod-template-podman (master) $ sudo !!
sudo podman run alpine
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a0d0a0d46f8b done  
Copying config 14119a10ab done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
WARN[0002] Failed to add conmon to cgroupfs sandbox cgroup: error creating cgroup for cpu: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/libpod_parent: read-only file system 
Error: creating cgroup directory `/sys/fs/cgroup/memory/libpod_parent/libpod-960cc7a4b619e79904b10a34b88605764dba42224423b5dc06c7609816386f6a`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```

From the debug info it shows conmon is failing as it does not having permissions. Anything that can be done?

If you fork the repo, you can modify the link: `https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/[yourusername]/gitpod-template-podman` to retry.

comments.body:
the first thing I'd check is why `/` is not mounted as shared.

Could you try `mount --make-rshared /` as root first?

Also looks like something in your setup is wrong.  Is /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid setup correctly for this user.
What does `podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map` show?

@giuseppe :-(

```
$ sudo mount --make-rshared /
mount: /: permission denied.
```

@rhatdan doesn't get that far :-(

```
$ podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers 
Error: cannot setup namespace using newuidmap: exit status 1
```

sounds like something on Gitpod restrics the usage.

Let's focus on the rootless usecase later. At the moment using:

```
gitpod /workspace/gitpod-template-podman (master) $ sudo podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
gitpod /workspace/gitpod-template-podman (master) $ sudo podman pull alpine
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a0d0a0d46f8b done  
Copying config 14119a10ab done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
14119a10abf4669e8cdbdff324a9f9605d99697215a0d21c360fe8dfa8471bab
gitpod /workspace/gitpod-template-podman (master) $ sudo podman run alpine
WARN[0000] Failed to add conmon to cgroupfs sandbox cgroup: error creating cgroup for blkio: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/blkio/libpod_parent: read-only file system 
Error: creating cgroup directory `/sys/fs/cgroup/hugetlb/libpod_parent/libpod-b64094f0505628bdf5eb3749cf0f8b267cb60f5c836eb39df7acba49562115ad`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```

> the first thing I'd check is why `/` is not mounted as shared.
> 
> Could you try `mount --make-rshared /` as root first?

I was having the same WARN and this command solves the issue under _Fedora Remix for WSL v34_.
Thanks!

@gbraad I think the error is coming from `crun` (https://github.com/containers/crun/blob/main/src/libcrun/cgroup.c#L587) . I wonder something is up with way the cgroup is mounted, permission issue or something entirely different with `gitpod` . 

Could you please paste content of  `mount`, `cat /proc/self/cgroup`,  `cat /proc/cgroups`  and `ls /sys/fs/cgroup`

If you click the link above you can actually run it yourself and inspect.
Else you have to wait till tomorrow
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Gitpod can now run Fedora images since the introduction of Imge Builder mk3.

Will update the repo and try again.
Ok reopen this issue if there is work required by podman.

This is still an issue, but this is on Gitpod to resolve. GitHub Codespaces does not have this issue. This can easily be tested with the https://github.com/gbraad/gitpod-template-fedora image and installing `dnf install -y podman`

/cc: @csweichel
